title: Destruction of Taris
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Devastation of Taris}}

{{Quote|Wipe this pathetic planet from the face of the galaxy!|Darth Malak|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Following the destruction of the ''[[Endar Spire]]'' by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] battle fleet in hopes of capturing [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi Padawan]]{{fact}} [[Bastila Shan]], the '''destruction of Taris''' was an event that took place during the height of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Intending to capture the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bastila Shan]], whose talents with [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] had been crucial to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] effort against the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] led by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Malak|Malak]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] set his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] around the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. After successfully [[Attack on the Endar Spire|ambushing]] the vessel under Shan's command, the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''Endar Spire'', [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] led by [[Darth Bandon]] boarded the [[Starship/Legends|ship]], intent on seizing her alive. However, Shan, [[Carth Onasi]] and the amnesic ex-[[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] were able to escape before the vessel's destruction. Afterwards, Malak ordered the conquest and [[Blockade/Legends|blockading]] of Taris in order to search for the Jedi. The [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] bearing Shan crashed in the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]], however, and she was taken [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] by the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]] [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]].

With support from the [[Hidden Beks]] [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] gang, Revan found and freed Shan. Impressed, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Canderous Ordo]] offered Revan an opportunity to leave the planet by stealing [[Exchange]] [[Crime lord/Legends|boss]] [[Davik Kang]]'s [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''. Accepting the offer, Revan raided the main [[Tarisian military base|Sith base]] to acquire codes to bypass the blockade; Ordo then brought him to Kang's [[Davik Kang's estate|estate]] to steal the ship. Malak, meanwhile, grew weary of the protracted search. Seeking to eliminate Shan, he ordered [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] to thoroughly [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombard]] the surface. As the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] fleet in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] repositioned itself, Ordo and Revan succeeded in accessing the ''Hawk''. However, they were confronted by Kang and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Calo Nord]]. After a brief firefight as the bombardment began, Kang was [[Death/Legends|killed]] and Nord incapacitated; with the way clear, the party boarded the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], picking up Onasi, Shan, [[Mission Vao]], [[Zaalbar]], and [[T3-M4]], and blasted off for [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The ''Ebon Hawk'' eluded a vanguard of attacking [[Sith fighter]]s before fleeing into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], en route to the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Despite their escape, the bombardment continued until the entire world was virtually scoured of life, killing billions of beings. However, Taris's ecosystem eventually recovered over the next three centuries, leading the Republic to [[Taris Resettlement Initiative|resettle]] the planet.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|Bastila's escape pod is away!|Carth Onasi|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
{{Main|Attack on the Endar Spire|Rescue of Bastila Shan}}
In an effort to capture the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bastila Shan]], whose mastery of [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] had served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] well in its fight against the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Malak|Malak]] had tracked her as she journeyed to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] aboard the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]''. With his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] waiting in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the planet to trap her vessel, Malak ordered a large number of [[Sith fighter]]s from his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruiser]] [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], to attack the ship as it was dragged out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], before boarding the captured ship. During [[Attack on the Endar Spire|the battle]] that followed, Shan managed to board an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], which crashed into the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]] of the planet. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Carth Onasi]], a veteran of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and a decorated war hero, and the recently [[Memory wipe/Legends|mind-wiped]] former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], were also aboard the doomed vessel at the time; after fending off or bypassing a number of [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] and [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who were part of the boarding party, the two met up on the starboard side of the ship and used the last escape pod to reach the [[Upper City]] of Taris moments before the ''Endar Spire'' was destroyed. After landing, Onasi pulled the unconscious Revan from the pod and, dragging him through the crowd of Upper City residents who had arrived to investigate, reached safety in an abandoned apartment.<ref name="KotOR" />

Realizing that Shan had not been captured or killed in the destruction of the ''Endar Spire'', Malak ordered an immediate conquest of the planet, instituting a massive [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in order to prevent all [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel to and from Taris. Shan, however, had been captured by a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] known as the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]], which were based in the decaying [[Lower City]]. The group offered Shan up as the prize for the winner of the [[Tarisian Season Opener]] [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop race]], the key event of the [[Galactic Swoop Racing Circuit]]. Revan and Onasi, with the assistance of the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Mission Vao]] and her [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion [[Zaalbar]], eventually [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|won the favor of a rival swoop gang]], the [[Hidden Beks]], led by [[Gadon Thek]], by returning a stolen [[prototype accelerator]]. As a condition of their agreement, Thek agreed to sponsor Revan for the race under the Bek banner. After Revan successfully beat the times of all other opponents, [[Brejik]], the leader of the Vulkars, retracted the prize, accusing the Beks of cheating by using the accelerator that the Vulkars had stolen. After freeing herself from Vulkar captivity, Shan intervened in the brief fight that followed, which ended in the gang leader's [[Death/Legends|death]].<ref name="KotOR" />

==The bombardment==
{{Quote|The search for Bastila is taking too long. We cannot risk her escaping Taris. Destroy the entire planet.|Darth Malak, to Admiral Karath|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
[[File:TarisDestroyed.jpg|thumb|left|300px|thumb|The destruction of Taris.]]
Malak, however, was growing impatient with his troops' inability to find Shan on the planet's surface. In an attempt to completely remove her from the playing field, denying her battle meditation to the Republic, he ordered [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] to begin an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the planet. Karath, hesitant at first, stated that it would take some time to properly arrange the fleet to initiate the operation. Karath also questioned Malak about those in the Sith military who had been stationed throughout the vast [[City/Legends|cityscape]]. Still angry, Malak ordered the preparations to begin at once and offered no care for the loss of life, regardless of allegiance. With the armada in place and ready, the bombardment began.<ref name="KotOR" /> The Taris government, along with representatives of every major science and industry, froze themselves in carbonite as the destruction began so that they could be saved. They were stored in an underground archive, where they were to remain until Taris was once again in Republic hands.<ref name="The Archive">{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|The Archive|Taris}}</ref>

Meanwhile, on the surface, Revan had teamed up with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Canderous Ordo]], who had a plan for getting off-world. In order to infiltrate the [[Tarisian military base|Sith military base]], in which the codes necessary to pass through the blockade were kept, he bade Revan acquire the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[T3-M4]] from [[Janice Nall]], the Twi'lek owner of a local [[Droid/Legends|droid]] dealership in the Upper City, in order to break into the base itself. With the assistance of T3, Revan successfully raided the base and secured the passage codes after a brief duel with the planet's [[Sith Governor]]. With the codes in hand, Ordo promised Revan the means of stealing the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', the recently acquired flagship of [[Davik Kang]], who was the head of the [[Exchange]]'s presence on Taris. Ordo brought Revan to Kang's [[Davik Kang's estate|estate]] in order to hijack the ''Hawk'', claiming that Revan was a person who might make a prime recruit. While attempting to steal the ship, having freed its former [[Hudrow|pilot]] and getting the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] security codes, Revan and Ordo were confronted by Kang and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Calo Nord]]. Kang was killed in the fight that followed; however, the bombardment had already begun. The hangar began to collapse, causing debris to fall on Nord, severely injuring him and preventing his activation of a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]. Revan then boarded the ''Ebon Hawk'', gathering all of his companions before fleeing the planet, even amidst the barrage of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire.<ref name="KotOR" />

[[File:Ebonhawk-flee.jpg|thumb|275px|thumb|[[Sith fighter]]s pursue the ''Ebon Hawk''.]]
After gathering all of his and Revan's companions aboard the ''Ebon Hawk'', Carth Onasi piloted the ship off the planet and up out of its [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity well]] in spite of the sheets of destructive energy raining down upon the surface as the bombardment continued. Once above Taris, however, the fleeing vessel soon encountered elements of the Sith fleet. Even though a half-[[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of Sith fighters attacked the ''Hawk'' as it continued to flee the battle, they were destroyed by Revan, who had manned the ship's dorsal-mounted [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] turret, giving Onasi a window in which to make the jump into hyperspace. The ''Ebon Hawk'' and its crew, at Shan's urging, then fled to the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the grassy world of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. After their escape, the bombardment continued, mercilessly pummeling the surface and reducing the planetwide cityscape to a smoking ruin and killing billions of innocent civilians.<ref name="KotOR" /> The planet's skyscrapers collapsed to the surface, including Taris's most lively cultural and business districts, which formed an area known as the [[Sinking City]].<ref name="Sinking City">{{TORcite|type=codex|Sinking City}}</ref>

==Aftermath==
===Initial effects===
{{Quote|You saw what his fleet did to Taris: there wasn't a building over two stories high left standing! They... they turned the planet into one big pile of rubble.|Carth Onasi|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
The Sith fleet decimated the surface of Taris, killing billions of its citizens, though the exact number of fatalities remained unknown.<ref name="casualties" /> The destruction was so vast that survival was unlikely for most residents, with the exception of the [[Outcasts (Taris)|Outcasts]]. Other than Revan and his companions, one notable survivor was Calo Nord. Nord was able to free himself from the debris that had trapped him and flee on his ship. He would later offer his services to Malak in a renewed attempt to hunt down Shan.<ref name="KotOR" />

The destruction of Taris became an important event that echoed throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Citizens of other planets, such as Dantooine, wondered if the Sith would attempt such an attack there, as well. Wherever Revan traveled, he encountered discussion about the event. Even among his companions, emotions ranged from shock, as exhibited by Mission Vao, to anger, as shown by [[Juhani]], a [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] Jedi from Taris who was off-world at the time. The destruction of the planet also affected commerce. Taris was still an important commercial and cultural center, as well as the hub of swoop racing throughout known space; the circuit was decimated by the losses suffered in the bombardment. Even though [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] had won the race, no one in the galaxy believed him because they believed that no one could have escaped that bombardment. Another immediate effect was the shortage of [[Tarisian ale]], which later became a potential business opportunity for Tarisian Twi'lek [[Griff Vao]], Mission's elder brother.<ref name="KotOR" />

The [[Outcasts (Taris)|Outcasts]], a group of Tarisians who lived in the Undercity prior to the bombardment, successfully reached their [[Promised Land]], which they discovered to be the original colony established by Taris's first settlers, despite the bombardment. Thanks to a [[rakghoul serum]] given to them by Revan, the "Promised Ones" were able to survive for many generations, but the serum eventually ran out, and the group was ultimately killed off by rakghouls and radiation poisoning.<ref name="Chasing History">{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|Chasing History|Taris}}</ref> The swoop racer [[Milos Brejik]] was among the few survivors of the bombardment, and he turned scavenger in the years afterwards, foraging for supplies and delivering them to the other survivors.<ref name="Brejik's Run">{{TORcite|type=codex|Brejik's Run}}</ref>

===Recovery===
In the centuries that followed the bombardment, Taris's ecosystem began to adapt to the toxic environment left by the former city-world and thrived in the absence of settlers: plants overgrew the wreckage of fallen skyscrapers, and animals repopulated the growing jungles as they adapted to the toxins and pollutants left in the wreckage.<ref name="Environment of Taris">{{TORcite|type=codex|Environment of Taris}}</ref> Many of the factories and production facilities, including the [[Taris ChemWorks Factory]],<ref name="Spilling">{{TORcite|type=heroic|Spilling Chemicals|Taris}}</ref> were damaged during the bombardment, releasing chemical and toxic waste into the environment. During the bombardment, a factory that was believed to belong to a Tarisian environmentalist named [[Ark Brell]] was destroyed and leaked chemical waste, forming an acid lake later termed [[Lake Brell]], and the area around the lake became known as the [[Brell Sediment]].<ref name="Brell Sediment">{{TORcite|type=codex|Brell Sediment}}</ref>
[[File:Taris_Resettlement.png|thumb|right|200px|The Taris Resettlement Initiative]]
Several ancient species that existed on Taris before the planet's initial colonization, such as the [[bogstalker]], were believed to be extinct until they were rediscovered when the Republic began to recolonize Taris. Bogstalkers in particular absorbed the pollutants from the bombardment, developing poisonous flesh over the centuries.<ref name="Bogstalker">{{TORcite|type=codex|Bogstalker}}</ref> The population of the supposedly extinct [[ferrazid hound]]s also recovered in the absence of Taris's urban environment, developing a robust population.<ref name="Ferrazid">{{TORcite|type=codex|Ferrazid Hound}}</ref> Unfortunately for the rest of the galaxy, Taris's rakghoul population was not completely wiped out in the bombardment, and the creatures thrived despite the absence of other sentients to infect—some rakghouls were even mutated by radiation and pollution left by the bombardment, growing stronger and more violent.<ref name="Mutations">{{TORcite|type=heroic|Mutations|Taris}}</ref>

An unexpected result of the rakghouls' isolation was the evolution of the [[nekghoul]], a subspecies of rakghoul that was approaching sentience and could use the Force.<ref name="Nekghoul">{{TORcite|type=codex|Nekghoul}}</ref> The [[tarsarian stomper]]—a species not native to Taris that had been kept in a number of Tarisian zoos prior to the bombardment—was released into the environment and initially thrived, but several stompers were infected by rakghouls and, thanks to a mutation in the rakghoul virus, developed into creatures that the Republic named [[tarsarian devourer]]s. The devourers quickly wiped out their stomper cousins and became the kings of the food chain on Taris, though the mutated virus in the devourers lost its ability to infect sentient species.<ref name="Tarsarian Devourer">{{TORcite|type=codex|Tarsarian Devourer}}</ref>

Around [[3648 BBY]],<ref name="Time">{{TOR_dates|1}} The ''[[Endar Rebirth]]'' was sent to Taris five years before the game, giving a date of [[3648 BBY]].</ref> during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the Republic and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]], the Republic established the [[Taris Reconstruction Authority]] which oversaw the [[Taris Resettlement Initiative|resettlement and reconstruction]] of Taris. In rebuilding Taris, which had been destroyed by Sith centuries earlier, the Republic sought to create a symbol of hope and steadfastness against the new Sith threat.<ref name="Taris">{{TORcite|type=codex|Taris}}</ref> The Republic established the [[Republic Tarisian Archival Database]], which sought to recover and preserve knowledge of Taris from before the bombardment.<ref name="The Archive" /> Republic archaeological salvage teams also cataloged the bodies discovered in the wreckage in hopes of identifying them.<ref name="Corpse">{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|The Corpse Counters|Taris}}</ref>

A rare cancer known as [[Colu's syndrome|Colu's Syndrome]] developed in the centuries after the bombardment; the cancer only affected the descendants of refugees from Taris.<ref name="Legacy of Death">{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|Legacy of Death|Taris}}</ref> Pirates also flocked to the devastated world, scavenging the wreckage and braving the planet's dangers in search of treasure and lost wealth.<ref name="Pirates">{{TORcite|type=codex|Pirates and Scavengers}}</ref> While the Reconstruction Authority made significant progress under [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Leontyne Saresh]],<ref name="Saresh">{{TORcite|type=codex|Governor Saresh}}</ref> the Sith Empire [[Invasion of Taris|invaded]] Taris in the early days of the [[Galactic War]] between the Republic and the Empire. The Empire razed the Reconstruction Authority's work, seeking to restore what they saw as a glorious monument to Darth Malak and the power of the Sith.<ref name="Taris" />

By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], nearly four thousand years after the bombardment, much of the planet had been rebuilt, once again becoming a prominent city-world in the galaxy, but the scars that had been left by Darth Malak and his armada still remained.<ref name="EaW">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''</ref> By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the planet was still a hub for many elements of galactic culture, including a small number of Mandalorians who had set up shop there.<ref name="ReferenceA">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The destruction of Taris, as it first appears in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', is depicted mostly as a series of cutscenes and scripted in-game sequences. The only real involvement by the player, other than securing the ''Ebon Hawk'' from Davik Kang in order to escape the planet, is taking up the laser cannon turret on the ''Hawk'' and shooting down a number of pursuing Sith fighters before making the jump to hyperspace en route to Dantooine.<ref name="KotOR" /> Content that was cut from the final game reveals that a large number of automated landing craft were sent by the Sith fleet to retrieve their ground troops; one of these was to allow the escape of [[Deadeye Duncan]], who was to have met Revan on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]] in order to ask permission to use the title of "[[Mysterious Stranger]]."<ref name="WRIK1R">{{WebCite|url=https://deadlystream.com/topic/3075-whats-restored-in-k1r/|text=What's Restored In K1R|author=Fair Strides|work=[https://deadlystream.com Deadly Stream]|archivedate=20250715194832}}</ref>

The battle is later indirectly referenced in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' and its expansion, [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption|''Forces of Corruption'']], which depicts a cityscape not unlike that of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], which is also depicted in the game.<ref name="EaW" /> In ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'', another indirect mention is made when [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] visits Taris in order to meet with a number of his Mandalorian allies.<ref name="ReferenceA" /> Most recently, when ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' was being written, the creators of the game wanted to return to Taris and use it as a focal point for the game. As a result, the destruction of the planet was an important factor into the creation of the landscape. The reestablishment of Taris also became a plot point in the game.<ref>{{TORweb|url=info/media/trailers/returning-taris|text=Video - Returning to Taris}}</ref><ref name="The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Old Republic">''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>
